reasoning: The question asks whether MCD's market close price on an unspecified list of resolution dates (inferred to be the close date of 2026-01-24) will be higher than on 2025-10-26. This represents a forecasting horizon of approximately 90 days (about 3 months). McDonald's is a low-beta, defensive stock with typical annualized volatility around 15-18% and a long-term expected total return of roughly 8% annualized. Over a 3-month period, the expected total return is around 1.9%. Subtracting one expected quarterly ex-dividend payout of ~$1.77 (yielding ~0.6%), the expected price return is roughly 1.3%. With a 3-month standard deviation of approximately 8%, the z-score for a price greater than the initial price is roughly 0.16 to 0.17. Looking at a standard normal distribution, this translates to a roughly 56-57% probability that the price will be strictly higher on the resolution date than the base date.
